transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Hi. I'm looking at my statement and there's a charge for thirty-nine pounds from a subscription I cancelled three months ago. They're still taking money.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Let me see. I can see the charge. Sometimes cancellations don't process immediately through payment systems and charges can come through for a cycle or two.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Three months is more than a cycle or two. Can I get a refund?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I can make a note on your account. Whether you get a refund would depend on the merchant's policy — you'd ideally raise it with them directly as well.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: But can you do anything from your end?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I can raise a dispute if you want.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Okay, I've noted a dispute. You'll hear back in due course.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I don't have one to give you right now. It should come through by email.

planted_violations: incomplete identity verification — name only, no date of birth
failed to proactively offer dispute resolution; required customer to push for it
dispute raised without providing case reference number or clear timeline
